checks.finding: acceptance-tests.md test table: every 'Expected' cell for TEST-001..TEST-004 ends with the identical literal 'Status: Planned' with no explanation of whether it is a test assertion or document-tracking metadata, creating a real risk that implementers build divergent RED/GREEN fixtures.

Goals, Non-goals, Edge Cases, and AC-001/AC-002 are mutually consistent on close reading; the quoted-text and multiple-lines edge cases are explicit, reconciled extensions of the masking rule, not conflicts with the 'other modifications still trip staleness' rule.

requirements.md Edge Case #2 (indented/bulleted 'Second Approval' mentions must NOT be deleted) has no corresponding negative-control test in TEST-002/acceptance-tests.md, despite Risks marking over-broad masking as a Critical mitigation target; Edge Case #5 (final line without trailing newline) also has no fixture despite being flagged as an sh/ps1 byte-identity risk.

Both listed Assumptions (pwsh 7.x availability, --registry entry point) cite existing repo precedent/usage, and Open Questions is explicitly closed with justification tied to Problems #2 and the Non-goals scope.

Roles and Permissions/Risks name a human-copy-only delivery boundary for applying the fix to protected gate scripts ('a botched live apply would break every gate'), but unlike the parallel write-restriction ('enforced by the hook guard; unchanged'), no enforcement mechanism is cited and no acceptance criterion verifies compliance with the human-copy/SHA-256-manifest mitigation.

The acceptance-table ambiguity, the missing security-relevant negative control, and the unverifiable delivery-safety mitigation together require a downstream design/task reviewer to invent product/process decisions rather than execute an unambiguous handoff.
